title: Package Installation Flow
activity_flow: START: execute() called with list of OperationInterface[]
Register SignalHandler for SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP → triggers runCleanup() on signal

BATCH SPLIT (download-modifying plugins): iterate operations; if InstallOperation/UpdateOperation targets a composer-plugin with extra[plugin-modifies-downloads]=true → flush current batch, isolate plugin in its own batch, start new batch
LOOP over batches → call downloadAndExecuteBatch(repo, batch, cleanupPromises, devMode, runScripts, downloadOnly, allOperations)
IF opType is alias (markAliasInstalled/markAliasUninstalled) → SKIP (no download needed)
Register cleanupPromise[index] = fn() calling installer->cleanup(opType, package, initialPackage)
IF opType != uninstall → call installer->download(package, initialPackage) → collect PromiseInterface
IF promises collected → waitOnPromises(promises) [blocks until all downloads complete, shows progress bar if applicable]
BATCH SPLIT (plugin execution order): re-batch operations; composer-plugin and composer-installer types get isolated batches (must be activated before dependents)
LOOP over execution sub-batches → call executeBatch(repo, subBatch, cleanupPromises, devMode, runScripts, allOperations)
IF alias opType → call this->{opType}(repo, operation) directly (markAliasInstalled / markAliasUninstalled) → CONTINUE
IF runScripts && eventDispatcher → dispatch PRE_PACKAGE_INSTALL | PRE_PACKAGE_UPDATE | PRE_PACKAGE_UNINSTALL event
installer->prepare(opType, package, initialPackage) [returns PromiseInterface or resolved null]
update: same-type → installer->update(repo, initial, target) + markForNotification; different-type → uninstall old then install new
Queue postExecCallback: fn() dispatching POST_PACKAGE_INSTALL | POST_PACKAGE_UPDATE | POST_PACKAGE_UNINSTALL (if runScripts)
IF promises → waitOnPromises(promises) [all prepare→install→cleanup→write chains run concurrently]
LOOP postExecCallbacks → invoke each (fire POST_* events sequentially)
IF repoUrl contains '%package%' (legacy API) → build per-package POST url, collect HTTP promise
ELSE build batch JSON POST with all packages + download metadata → collect HTTP promise

key_methods.execute: public execute(repo, operations[], devMode=true, runScripts=true, downloadOnly=false): void
key_methods.downloadAndExecuteBatch: private downloadAndExecuteBatch(repo, operations[], &cleanupPromises[], devMode, runScripts, downloadOnly, allOperations[]): void
key_methods.executeBatch: private executeBatch(repo, operations[], cleanupPromises[], devMode, runScripts, allOperations[]): void
key_methods.waitOnPromises: private waitOnPromises(promises[]): void — calls loop->wait(); shows ConsoleIO ProgressBar when count>1 and non-CI non-debug
key_methods.install: public install(repo, InstallOperation): ?PromiseInterface — calls installer->install() + markForNotification()
key_methods.update: public update(repo, UpdateOperation): ?PromiseInterface — same-type update or uninstall+install chain
key_methods.notifyInstalls: public notifyInstalls(io): void — HTTP POST to notification URLs
key_methods.runCleanup: private runCleanup(cleanupPromises[]): void — aborts jobs then waits on cleanup promises
key_methods.getInstaller: public getInstaller(type): InstallerInterface — lookup with cache
key_methods.ensureBinariesPresence: public ensureBinariesPresence(package): void — calls BinaryPresenceInterface->ensureBinariesPresence() if supported

download_manager_methods.download: public download(package, targetDir, prevPackage=null): PromiseInterface — resolves source/dist preference, calls downloader->download() with fallback retry logic
download_manager_methods.prepare: public prepare(type, package, targetDir, prevPackage=null): PromiseInterface
download_manager_methods.update: public update(initial, target, targetDir): PromiseInterface — same downloader type: update(); different type: remove+install
download_manager_methods.cleanup: public cleanup(type, package, targetDir, prevPackage=null): PromiseInterface
download_manager_methods.getAvailableSources: private getAvailableSources(package, prevPackage): array — returns ordered ['dist','source'] or ['source','dist'] based on preferences

branching_conditions: plugin-modifies-downloads=true → isolate in its own download batch
downloadOnly=true → skip execution phase, run cleanup only
opType is alias → skip async promise chain, call directly
package.type == composer-plugin|composer-installer → isolate in its own execution sub-batch
runScripts && eventDispatcher != null → dispatch PRE/POST events
initialType != targetType during update → uninstall old installer + install with new installer
sourceFallback=true and RuntimeException (non-irrecoverable) → retry with next available source
key_relationships: InstallationManager composes Loop (for async promise waiting)
InstallationManager depends_on InstallerInterface (per package type, cached)
InstallationManager depends_on EventDispatcher (optional, for PRE/POST events)
InstallerInterface delegates downloads to DownloadManager internally
DownloadManager composes DownloaderInterface[] (keyed by type: zip, git, svn, etc.)
DownloadManager uses Filesystem for directory creation
Loop wraps ReactPHP event loop + HttpDownloader
important_notes: Two-phase async pipeline: (1) all downloads fire concurrently via React promises collected per batch, waitOnPromises blocks; (2) execution (prepare→install→cleanup→write) chains run concurrently within sub-batches. Plugin packages that modify downloads are always isolated in their own top-level batch so they are installed first. Composer-plugin/installer types are isolated in execution sub-batches to ensure plugin activation order. Signal handling ensures cleanup runs on SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGHUP. Notification HTTP calls are fire-and-forget with swallowed exceptions.
